 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","title":"Implement theme provider and dark mode toggle","description":"**Details:**
- ThemeProvider wrapping app for dark/light mode
- Persists preference to localStorage
- Respects system preference on first visit
- Dark mode toggle in Nav component
- CSS variables switch based on theme

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
'use client';
import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react';

type Theme = 'light' | 'dark' | 'system';

export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [theme, setTheme] = useState<Theme>('system');
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const stored = localStorage.getItem('theme') as Theme;
    if (stored) setTheme(stored);
  }, []);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const root = document.documentElement;
    const isDark = theme === 'dark' || 
      (theme === 'system' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches);
    root.classList.toggle('dark', isDark);
    localStorage.setItem('theme', theme);
  }, [theme]);
  
  return (
    <ThemeContext.Provider value={{ theme, setTheme }}>
      {children}
    </ThemeContext.Provider>
  );
}
```

**Toggle Component:**
- Sun/Moon icon button
- Cycles through light → dark → system
- Tooltip showing current mode

**Location:** 
- apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx

**Reasoning:** Dark mode is expected in developer tools. System preference respect shows attention to detail. Persistent preference provides consistent UX.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:00.527000897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ThemeProvider with light/dark/system modes, ThemeToggle component, integrated into Providers.tsx.  {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4q8k","title":"EPIC: CLI Authentication & Premium Integration","description":"## Overview
Add optional authentication to the `jfp` CLI tool, allowing users to seamlessly access their premium account data including custom prompts, collections, saved/bookmarked prompts, and notes.

## Why This Matters
The CLI is the primary interface for power users and AI agents. By adding authentication:
- Premium users get their full library in the CLI
- Users can access their collections and saved prompts
- The "swap meet" community prompts become available
- Notes and annotations sync across devices
- Search spans all content (public + personal)

## Design Principles

### 1. Transparent for Non-Users
- All existing functionality works WITHOUT login
- No nag screens, no forced auth, no degraded experience
- Login is purely additive - it unlocks MORE, never restricts
- Clear, honest messaging about what premium provides

### 2. Works Everywhere
- Local machines: Browser-based OAuth with auto-callback
- Remote/SSH: Device code flow (6-character code)
- Headless servers: Same device code flow
- CI/CD: Token-based auth via environment variable

### 3. Secure & Private
- Tokens stored securely in user's config directory
- Short-lived access tokens with refresh capability
- No passwords stored locally
- Easy revocation via web dashboard

## Authentication Flows

### Flow 1: Local Machine (Default)
```
User runs: jfp login
    ↓
CLI starts local HTTP server on random port (e.g., 19847)
    ↓
CLI opens browser to: https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com/cli/auth?port=19847
    ↓
User completes Google OAuth on premium site
    ↓
Premium site redirects to: http://localhost:19847/callback?token=xxx
    ↓
CLI receives token, stores it, shows success message
    ↓
Local server shuts down
```

### Flow 2: Remote Machine (--remote or auto-detected)
```
User runs: jfp login --remote  (or CLI detects no display)
    ↓
CLI requests device code from: POST /api/cli/device-code
    ↓
API returns: { device_code: "abc123", user_code: "XYZW-1234", verification_url: "..." }
    ↓
CLI shows: "Visit https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com/cli/verify and enter code: XYZW-1234"
    ↓
User visits URL, logs in with Google, enters code
    ↓
Meanwhile, CLI polls: POST /api/cli/device-token with device_code
    ↓
Once user completes, API returns access token
    ↓
CLI stores token, shows success
```

### Flow 3: CI/CD / Programmatic
```
User sets: JFP_TOKEN=xxx in environment
    ↓
CLI detects env var, uses it for all authenticated requests
    ↓
No interactive login needed
```

## New CLI Commands

### `jfp login`
- Initiates authentication flow
- Options: `--remote` (force device code flow)
- Stores credentials in `~/.config/jfp/credentials.json`

### `jfp logout`
- Clears stored credentials
- Optionally revokes token on server

### `jfp whoami`
- Shows current authentication status
- Displays: email, tier, token expiry

### `jfp list --mine`
- Lists only user's custom prompts

### `jfp list --saved`
- Lists user's bookmarked/saved prompts

### `jfp collections`
- Lists user's collections
- `jfp collections <name>` shows prompts in collection

### `jfp notes <prompt-id>`
- Shows notes for a prompt
- `jfp notes <prompt-id> --add "note text"` adds a note

## API Endpoints Needed (Premium Site)

### Auth Endpoints
- `POST /api/cli/device-code` - Initiate device code flow
- `POST /api/cli/device-token` - Exchange device code for token
- `POST /api/cli/token/refresh` - Refresh access token
- `POST /api/cli/token/revoke` - Revoke token (logout)

### Data Endpoints (Authenticated)
- `GET /api/cli/me` - Current user info
- `GET /api/cli/prompts` - All prompts (public + user's)
- `GET /api/cli/prompts/mine` - User's custom prompts only
- `GET /api/cli/prompts/saved` - User's saved/bookmarked prompts
- `GET /api/cli/collections` - User's collections
- `GET /api/cli/collections/:id` - Collection details with prompts
- `GET /api/cli/notes/:promptId` - Notes for a prompt
- `POST /api/cli/notes/:promptId` - Add note to prompt

## Token Format
Using JWT with claims:
- `sub`: User ID
- `email`: User email
- `tier`: Subscription tier (free/premium)
- `exp`: Expiration time
- `iat`: Issued at time

## Credential Storage
Location: `~/.config/jfp/credentials.json`
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJ...",
  "refresh_token": "xxx",
  "expires_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "tier": "premium"
}
```

## Success Criteria
- `jfp login` works on local machines with browser
- `jfp login --remote` works on headless/SSH machines
- Authenticated users see their prompts in `jfp list`
- `jfp search` finds results in personal prompts
- Token refresh happens automatically
- Logout clears all credentials
- Non-authenticated users have identical experience to before","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:08:45.460885074-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:08:45.460885074-05:00"}
+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4szj","title":"Public site: add subtle Pro teaser block + FAQ entry","description":"## Goal
Add a small, tasteful Pro teaser that explains the value in 1–2 lines and links to the Pro site, plus a short FAQ entry clarifying that the free site is fully open-source and Pro is optional.

## Where
- Landing page content component (likely `apps/web/src/app/page.tsx` or a shared marketing section).
- Footer or near the lower third of the page (below core browsing/search content).

## UX Requirements
- Keep it **subtle** and **non-intrusive**.
- Maintain the free-first tone: “Everything here is free; Pro adds personal features.”
- Compact, visually light (e.g., small card, horizontal callout, or a muted banner).

## Copy Draft
- Header: “Pro mode (optional)” or “Upgrade to Pro”
- Body (1–2 sentences):
  - “Keep your favorites, add notes, and access early prompts. Everything on the public site stays free and open-source.”
- Button: “See Pro features” -> `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`

## FAQ Entry (if FAQ exists)
- **Q:** Is the public site free and open-source?
- **A:** Yes. The core library and browsing experience are open-source. Pro is optional and adds personal features like bookmarks, notes, and early-access prompts.

## Implementation Notes
- Use existing UI primitives (Card / Callout / Banner) to stay consistent.
- No new dependencies.
- Keep typography smaller than the hero content.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A subtle Pro teaser appears on the landing page.
- A short FAQ entry explains free vs. Pro.
- All links point to `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`.
